title: Ukraine Strikes Tuapse Oil Refinery in 'Volcano' Operation
detail: Ukrainian long-range drone forces struck the Tuapse oil refinery in Russia's Krasnodar Krai overnight, setting off massive fires described by witnesses as turning the facility into a 'volcano.' The Tuapse refinery is one of Russia's largest Black Sea-region oil processing facilities, handling approximately 12 million tons of crude annually. The strike expanded Ukraine's campaign against Russian energy export infrastructure beyond the Baltic ports and inland refineries targeted in late March. European partners including Germany (€300M), Norway (€560M), Netherlands (€248M), and Belgium (€85M) pledged new drone funding for Ukraine on the same day.

media.caption: Tuapse oil refinery ablaze after Ukrainian drone strike along Russia's Black Sea coast

title: Russia Threatens European Defense Contractors With Published Target List
detail: Russian security officials published a list of European defense contractor facilities as alleged potential military targets — an unprecedented coercive move against nations funding Ukraine's drone warfare capabilities. The threat directly followed European partners pledging hundreds of millions of euros in new drone support for Ukraine. Analysts assessed the threat as coercive signaling rather than credible military intent, but noted it significantly escalated Russia-NATO tensions. Ukraine meanwhile achieved 132 combat engagements on the day, with Russia deploying 172 drones and 1 missile.

media.caption: Russia issues unprecedented threats against European defense firms backing Ukraine's drone campaign
